reasoning: The question asks if Magnus Carlsen's FIDE ranking in early 2026 will be as high or higher (i.e., equal or better, meaning a numerical value ≤) than his ranking in November 2025. Given that he has held the #1 ranking continuously since 2011 and historically maintains a significant rating gap over his closest competitors, he is exceedingly likely to be ranked #1 in November 2025 and to retain that #1 ranking through February 2026. A 3-month window provides very little time for him to be overtaken, and his level of classical chess activity minimizes the risk of him dropping off the FIDE active list. Even if he were to drop to #2 by November 2025, the criteria would be satisfied as long as he does not drop further to #3 by February 2026. Therefore, the probability is very high.
